summarization.a: The figure displays infrared absorption spectra from 2500 to 4000 cm⁻¹ at 300°C, comparing TMA (a) and HF (b) samples.

a.question: Does observing the absorbance in the range of 2500 to 4000cm-1 provide more information about the ALE mechanism compared to 800 to 1300cm-1?
What happens after TMA exposure?
a.answer: After the TMA exposure,  an increase in absorbance is observed at ∼2900 and 2950 cm−1 .

a.question: What forms new species and what removes species?
a.answer: After TMA exposure, n increase in absorbance is observed at ∼2900 and 2950 cm−1, this absorbance gain is in accord with the formation of AlF2(CH3)* or −OAl(CH3)2* species .  After the HF exposure, an absorbance decrease at ∼2900 and ∼2950 cm−1 is consistent with the removal of the previously added AlCH3* species

a.question: Does observing the IR absorbance at various spectra help with the understanding of the ALE mechanism?
